entries.finding: No general-purpose secrets redaction exists in the codebase. 14 narrow-scope mechanisms exist but none cover tool output, agent messages, file reads, or subprocess env.
sanitizedProcessEnv in packages/core/src/util/opencode-process.ts is MISNAMED — performs zero sanitization, passes ALL env vars including secrets to subprocesses.
Sidecar password (OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD) injected into process.env in sidecar.ts:103-105, then passed to utilityProcess.fork in server.ts:76 with ALL env vars via createSidecarEnv().
Enterprise storage (packages/enterprise/src/core/storage.ts:67-83) reads AWS access keys/secret keys from process.env with non-null assertions and no validation.
Provider credentials written back into process.env in multiple places: AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK (provider.ts:301), AICORE_SERVICE_KEY (provider.ts:549), OPENCODE_CONSOLE_TOKEN (config.ts:689).
Provider auto-detection (packages/core/src/plugin/env.ts) scans ALL provider env keys and auto-enables providers if matching env var exists.
Config variable substitution supports {env:VAR_NAME} syntax (config/variable.ts:37) — can read ANY env var into runtime config.
No file-read security exists: no is_likely_binary, no resolve_safe_path, no MAX_TEXT_FILE, no SENSITIVE_READ_PATTERNS.
No bash command security: no rerouting, no dangerous pattern detection, no command substitution blocking.
Debug packet config redaction (debug-packet.ts:124) uses key-name-only matching — values could still leak if keys are unknown.
Session export sanitization (session-export.ts:32) does not scan message content — secrets in conversation history could leak in exports.
Build-time Sentry auth token read in electron.vite.config.ts:18 — token could leak through CI logs or build artifacts.
GitHub token in release script (finalize-latest-json.ts:29) — read from GH_TOKEN/GITHUB_TOKEN with no validation.
Hardcoded GitHub OAuth client ID in app-config.ts:1 — low risk (GitHub client IDs are public) but sets bad precedent.
